mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods show no significant outperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals tightening labor markets, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low UNRATE regimes show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary monetary policy, reducing real interest rates and boosting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no positive correlation with joint CPI/FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 115 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, driving corporate margins and equity returns, leading to positive S&P 500 performance over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high INDPRO regimes show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1000 thousand units indicates weak demand for construction, reducing related sector earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low HOUST regimes show positive or neutral performance (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 140,000 jobs signals strong labor market dynamics, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenue, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS regimes show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in RSAFS (consumer credit) and HOUST (housing starts) indicate weakening demand across sectors, reducing corporate cash flows and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no negative correlation with joint RSAFS/HOUST declines (Rank IC > -0.05).
